Bottle_gourd___Anthracnose.name: Bottle_gourd___Anthracnose
Bottle_gourd___Anthracnose.description: Disease caused by Colletotrichum species. It produces dark, sunken spots on leaves, stems, and fruits. The spots may enlarge and cause the leaves to dry and fall prematurely, reducing plant productivity.
remedies: Remove and destroy infected leaves and fruits.
Apply fungicides such as Mancozeb or Carbendazim.
Spray Copper oxychloride at recommended doses.
precautions: Do not work in fields when plants are wet.
Use clean tools to avoid spreading spores
prevention: Maintain proper spacing for air circulation.
Practice crop rotation with non-cucurbit crops

Bottle_gourd___Downey_mildew.name: Bottle_gourd___Downey_mildew
Bottle_gourd___Downey_mildew.description: Downy mildew is a fungal-like disease caused by Pseudoperonospora cubensis. It appears as yellow patches on the upper surface of leaves and grey-purple mold on the underside.
remedies: Spray fungicides such as Metalaxyl, Mancozeb, or Chlorothalonil.
precautions: Avoid excessive humidity in the crop field.
Water plants early in the morning so leaves dry quickly.
prevention: Ensure good air circulation between plants.

Bottle_gourd___fresh_leaf.name: Bottle_gourd___fresh_leaf
Bottle_gourd___fresh_leaf.description: The plant is healthy with no visible disease symptoms. Leaves appear green, fresh, and free from spots or discoloration.

Brinjal___Cercospora Leaf Spot.name: Brinjal___Cercospora Leaf Spot
Brinjal___Cercospora Leaf Spot.description: Cercospora leaf spot is a fungal disease caused by Cercospora melongenae. It causes small circular brown spots with grey centers on leaves, which may lead to leaf drop and reduced yield.

Brinjal___healthy.name: Brinjal___healthy
Brinjal___healthy.description: The brinjal plant is healthy with no signs of disease. Leaves are green and show normal growth.

Capsicum___Bacterial_spot.name: Capsicum___Bacterial_spot
Capsicum___Bacterial_spot.description: Bacterial spot is caused by Xanthomonas bacteria. It appears as small dark spots on leaves and fruits, which may enlarge and cause defoliation.

Capsicum___healthy.name: Capsicum___healthy
Capsicum___healthy.description: The capsicum plant is healthy with vibrant green leaves and no disease symptoms.

Cassava___bacterial_blight.name: Cassava___bacterial_blight
Cassava___bacterial_blight.description: Cassava bacterial blight is caused by Xanthomonas axonopodis. It leads to leaf wilting, yellowing, and dieback of shoots, which can significantly reduce yield.
precautions: Avoid using infected cuttings for planting.

Cassava___brown_streak_disease.name: Cassava___brown_streak_disease
Cassava___brown_streak_disease.description: Cassava brown streak disease is a viral disease that causes yellow patches on leaves and brown streaks in stems and roots, leading to root rot and yield loss.
precautions: Monitor fields regularly for early symptoms.

Cassava___green_mottle.name: Cassava___green_mottle
Cassava___green_mottle.description: Green mottle disease causes irregular green and yellow mottling on cassava leaves. It affects plant growth and reduces crop yield.
remedies: Control insect vectors such as whiteflies.

Cassava___healthy.name: Cassava___healthy
Cassava___healthy.description: Cassava plants are healthy and show normal leaf color and growth without disease symptoms.

Cassava___mosaic_disease.name: Cassava___mosaic_disease
Cassava___mosaic_disease.description: Cassava mosaic disease is caused by viruses transmitted by whiteflies. It causes mosaic patterns, leaf distortion, and stunted plant growth.

Coffee___healthy.name: Coffee___healthy
Coffee___healthy.description: The coffee plant is healthy with green leaves and normal growth. There are no visible symptoms of disease or pest damage.

Coffee___red_spider_mite.name: Coffee___red_spider_mite
Coffee___red_spider_mite.description: Red spider mites are tiny pests that feed on the underside of coffee leaves. They cause yellow speckles, leaf discoloration, and webbing, leading to reduced photosynthesis.
remedies: Wash leaves with water to remove mites.
precautions: Avoid excessive use of chemical pesticides that kill beneficial insects.
prevention: Encourage natural predators such as lady beetles.

Coffee___rust.name: Coffee___rust
Coffee___rust.description: Coffee rust is a fungal disease caused by Hemileia vastatrix. It produces orange powder-like spots on the underside of leaves, leading to defoliation and yield loss.
remedies: Apply fungicides such as Copper fungicides or Hexaconazole.

Cucumber___Bacterial_Wilt.name: Cucumber___Bacterial_Wilt
Cucumber___Bacterial_Wilt.description: Bacterial wilt is caused by Erwinia tracheiphila and transmitted by cucumber beetles. Infected plants wilt suddenly and eventually die.
precautions: Avoid planting cucumbers near infected plants.
prevention: Apply row covers to prevent beetle infestation.

Cucumber___Gummy_Stem_Blight.name: Cucumber___Gummy_Stem_Blight
Cucumber___Gummy_Stem_Blight.description: A fungal disease caused by Didymella bryoniae. It causes brown lesions on stems and leaves with gummy exudates.

Grape___Leaf_blight.name: Grape___Leaf_blight
Grape___Leaf_blight.description: Leaf blight causes brown lesions on grape leaves which eventually lead to leaf drying and reduced plant vigor.
remedies: Apply fungicides such as Mancozeb or Copper fungicide.

Grape___black_measles.name: Grape___black_measles
Grape___black_measles.description: Black measles (Esca disease) causes dark spots on leaves and fruits. It affects vine health and reduces yield.

Grape___black_rot.name: Grape___black_rot
Grape___black_rot.description: Black rot is a fungal disease causing brown spots on leaves and black shriveled fruits.
remedies: Spray fungicides like Mancozeb or Myclobutanil.

Lemon_Bacterial_Blight.name: Lemon_Bacterial_Blight
Lemon_Bacterial_Blight.description: A bacterial disease causing leaf spots, yellowing, and twig dieback in lemon plants.

Lemon_Citrus_Canker.name: Lemon_Citrus_Canker
Lemon_Citrus_Canker.description: Citrus canker causes raised corky lesions on leaves, stems, and fruits, reducing fruit quality.
precautions: Avoid movement of infected plant materials.

Lemon___Curl_Virus.name: Lemon___Curl_Virus
Lemon___Curl_Virus.description: Leaf curl virus causes leaf curling, distortion, and stunted growth in lemon plants.
remedies: Control insect vectors such as aphids.
precautions: Do not touch infected plants unnecessarily.

Lemon___Sooty_Mould.name: Lemon___Sooty_Mould
Lemon___Sooty_Mould.description: Sooty mold appears as black fungal growth on leaves due to honeydew secreted by insects like aphids.
remedies: Wash leaves with mild soap solution.

Onion___Alternaria_D.name: Onion___Alternaria_D
Onion___Alternaria_D.description: Alternaria disease causes dark concentric rings on onion leaves leading to leaf drying.
precautions: Do not irrigate late in the evening.

Onion___Healthy.name: Onion___Healthy
Onion___Healthy.description: The onion plant is healthy and shows no signs of disease.
prevention: Maintain good soil fertility and irrigation.

Onion___Purple_blotch.name: Onion___Purple_blotch
Onion___Purple_blotch.description: Purple blotch causes purple lesions on onion leaves which enlarge and cause leaf death.
remedies: Spray fungicides like Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb.

Onion___Virosis-D.name: Onion___Virosis-D
Onion___Virosis-D.description: Viral disease causing yellow streaks, stunted growth, and reduced bulb size.
precautions: Avoid excessive pesticide use that kills beneficial insects.

Potato___pests.name: Potato___pests
Potato___pests.description: Pests such as aphids, beetles, and caterpillars attack potato plants causing leaf damage and reduced yield.
remedies: Use insecticides or neem oil sprays.

Rice___bacterial_blight.name: Rice___bacterial_blight
Rice___bacterial_blight.description: A bacterial disease causing yellowing and drying of rice leaves.

Rice___blast.name: Rice___blast
Rice___blast.description: Rice blast is a fungal disease causing diamond-shaped lesions on leaves.

Rice___brown_spot.name: Rice___brown_spot
Rice___brown_spot.description: Brown spot causes circular brown lesions on rice leaves.

Rice___tungro.name: Rice___tungro
Rice___tungro.description: Tungro is a viral disease transmitted by leafhoppers causing yellow-orange discoloration and stunted growth.

Rose___healthy.name: Rose___healthy
Rose___healthy.description: Rose plant is healthy with green leaves and normal flowering.

Rose___rust.name: Rose___rust
Rose___rust.description: Rust causes orange powdery spots on rose leaves leading to leaf drop.
remedies: Apply fungicides such as Sulfur fungicide.

Rose___slug_sawfly.name: Rose___slug_sawfly
Rose___slug_sawfly.description: Slug sawfly larvae feed on rose leaves causing skeletonized leaves.

Sugercane___healthy.name: Sugercane___healthy
Sugercane___healthy.description: Sugarcane plant is healthy with strong stems and green leaves.

Sugercane___mosaic.name: Sugercane___mosaic
Sugercane___mosaic.description: Mosaic virus causes mosaic patterns on sugarcane leaves.

Sugercane___red_rot.name: Sugercane___red_rot
Sugercane___red_rot.description: Red rot is a fungal disease causing red discoloration inside stems.

Sugercane___rust.name: Sugercane___rust
Sugercane___rust.description: Rust disease produces reddish-brown pustules on leaves.

Sugercane___yellow_leaf.name: Sugercane___yellow_leaf
Sugercane___yellow_leaf.description: Yellow leaf disease causes yellowing of leaf midrib and reduced growth.

Watermelon___downy_mildew.name: Watermelon___downy_mildew
Watermelon___downy_mildew.description: Downy mildew causes yellow spots on leaves with gray mold underneath

Watermelon___mosaic_virus.name: Watermelon___mosaic_virus
Watermelon___mosaic_virus.description: Mosaic virus causes mottled yellow-green patterns on watermelon leaves and distorted fruits.
precautions: Control aphids which spread the virus.
